profile.tagline: Real-time decoded call trace API for every Base transaction — no ABI hunting, no raw hex.
profile.full_description: Callpath provides a REST and WebSocket API that accepts any Base transaction hash, block range, or contract address and returns fully decoded call traces with human-readable function names, typed arguments, internal calls, emitted events, and revert reasons. The API is backed by a live ABI registry that auto-indexes verified contracts from Basescan and accepts community-submitted ERC-7730-compatible JSON metadata stored on IPFS. For unverified contracts, Callpath runs a bytecode fingerprint matcher against a 4-byte selector database to apply known ERC patterns and returns partial decoding with per-field confidence scores. ERC-4337 UserOp bundles are unpacked into individual decoded operations with per-op gas accounting and revert attribution. This gives portfolio trackers, AI agent observability layers, and wallet UX teams structured transaction semantics without operating their own archival node and decoder stack.
profile.team_description: The founding team has backgrounds in EVM tooling and backend infrastructure, with prior experience building indexing pipelines for on-chain analytics platforms and contributing to open-source ABI decoding libraries. They have shipped production-grade RPC middleware used by wallet teams on Ethereum mainnet.

roadmap.goal: Ship a working prototype that decodes verified-contract transactions on Base via REST endpoint.
deliverables: POST /trace endpoint accepting a Base tx hash and returning a typed call tree for Basescan-verified contracts
Basescan ABI indexer that polls verified contracts every block and persists ABIs to PostgreSQL
Internal testbed covering 10 high-volume Base protocols (Uniswap v3, Aerodrome, Base Bridge) with assertion-based decode accuracy checks

roadmap.goal: Add bytecode fingerprint decoding, ERC-4337 UserOp unpacking, and WebSocket streaming for public developer access.
deliverables: Bytecode fingerprint matcher integrated with 4-byte selector DB, returning per-field confidence scores for unverified contracts
ERC-4337 UserOp bundle decoder that explodes bundled operations into individual decoded calls with per-op gas and revert attribution
WebSocket subscription endpoint allowing developers to stream decoded call events for any contract address in real time

roadmap.goal: Launch public API with metered pricing, community ABI submission pipeline, and ERC-7730 on-chain metadata support.
deliverables: Public API portal with API key issuance, usage dashboards, and tiered rate limits (free / pro / enterprise)
Community ABI submission flow accepting ERC-7730-compatible JSON metadata pinned to IPFS and indexed within one block confirmation
Block-range batch endpoint (GET /traces?from=&to=) enabling historical analytics queries across up to 1000 blocks per request

original_idea.tagline: Real-time decoded API for every Base contract interaction — no ABI hunting, no raw hex.
original_idea.problem: In 2026, Base processes over 5M transactions daily across hundreds of deployed protocols, but developers building on top of Base — dashboards, analytics tools, AI agents, wallets — still have to manually source ABIs, decode calldata, and stitch together trace trees from raw RPC responses. There is no unified, maintained API that returns human-readable, structured call traces for any Base transaction or block in real time.
original_idea.solution: Callpath exposes a single REST and WebSocket API that accepts any Base transaction hash, block range, or contract address and returns fully decoded call traces — function names, typed arguments, internal calls, emitted events, and revert reasons — resolved against a live ABI registry that auto-indexes verified contracts from Basescan and community-submitted ABIs stored as ERC-7730-compatible JSON metadata on-chain. For unverified contracts, Callpath uses a bytecode fingerprint matcher to apply known proxy and ERC patterns (ERC-20, ERC-721, ERC-4337 UserOp bundles) and returns best-effort partial decoding with confidence scores.
original_idea.target_user: Developers building Base-native products — portfolio trackers, AI agent observability layers, on-chain analytics dashboards, and wallet UX teams — who need structured, real-time transaction semantics without running their own archival node and decoder stack.
original_idea.unique_insight: The real bottleneck for Base ecosystem tooling is not compute or storage — it is the absence of a maintained, queryable semantic layer that turns raw EVM execution into structured data that application code can actually consume.
core_features: Single-endpoint decoded trace API: POST a tx hash, receive a fully typed call tree with human-readable function names, argument values, and child calls
Live ABI registry auto-synced from Basescan verified contracts and community ERC-7730 submissions, updated every block
Bytecode fingerprint decoder for unverified contracts — matches known selectors and ERC patterns with per-field confidence scoring
ERC-4337 UserOp unpacking: explode bundled userOps into individual decoded operations with per-op gas and revert attribution
WebSocket stream subscription: subscribe to any contract address and receive decoded call events in real time as blocks land
original_idea.why_now: Base crossed 5M daily transactions in early 2026 and is now the primary deployment target for AI agent wallets and ERC-4337 smart accounts — both of which generate deeply nested, hard-to-decode call trees that existing block explorers and raw RPC endpoints cannot serve at API scale. The tooling gap is actively blocking a generation of Base-native product teams.
